message:
fix(review-r5/tools): clamp contact TTLs + warn-on-clamp in renew + clippy useless_conversion

Three related fixes surfaced during a fresh-eyes review of the
`mcp-agent-mail-tools` crate (build_slots.rs, macros.rs, contacts.rs,
reservations.rs, reservation_index.rs):

1. `contacts::request_contact` and `contacts::respond_contact` previously
   took caller-supplied `ttl_seconds` and only clamped the lower bound
   via `.max(60)`. The upper bound was unbounded, so an `i64::MAX`
   input would flow into `queries::request_contact` where
   `now.saturating_add(ttl_seconds.saturating_mul(1_000_000))` pins
   `expires_ts` at `i64::MAX` and the contact link is effectively
   permanent. Every other TTL-accepting tool in the crate
   (`file_reservation_paths`, `acquire_build_slot`,
   `renew_build_slot`, `macro_file_reservation_cycle`,
   `macro_contact_handshake`) already uses `.clamp(60, 31_536_000)`
   (1 year) — `macro_contact_handshake` even comments
   "consistent with other macros", but the tools it delegates to
   didn't actually enforce that. Switch both request/respond to
   `.clamp(60, 31_536_000)` with the same warn-on-clamp logging
   pattern the sibling tools use.

2. `reservations::renew_file_reservations` clamped `extend_seconds`
   via `.clamp(60, 31_536_000)` but silently — `file_reservation_paths`
   and `macro_file_reservation_cycle` emit a `tracing::warn!` when
   either bound is tripped so callers can see that their value was
   modified. Add the matching warn pair so the three sites behave
   consistently.

3. `identity.rs:1902` (test-only) had a pre-existing
   `entity: "agent".into()` that tripped `-D clippy::useless_conversion`
   under `--all-targets` (the `NotFound::entity` field is
   `&'static str`, so `.into()` on a literal is a no-op). Remove
   the `.into()`. This is the only hunk staged from `identity.rs`;
   other in-flight edits in that file are from a concurrent agent
   and are intentionally left uncommitted.

Verification: `rch exec -- cargo check -p mcp-agent-mail-tools` and
`rch exec -- cargo clippy -p mcp-agent-mail-tools --all-targets -- -D warnings`
both clean. `cargo test -p mcp-agent-mail-tools --lib` on rch was
still queued on ts2 at the ~35m mark when this commit was prepared —
the lib tests serialize through `with_serialized_reservations`, and
the review-r4-builds thread has already flagged the broader suite
as flaky on this checkout. These changes are scoped to TTL-clamp
helpers and test-only annotations, so they should not affect suite
outcomes.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
